#load packages
```{r}
install.packages("purrr")
library(purrr)  # for map functions
install.packages("tidyr")
library(tidyr)  # for nest and pivot_wider
```


#post-processing
```{r}
# ============================================
#     EMOS POST-PROCESSING - FIXED VERSION
# ============================================

install.packages("dplyr")
library(dplyr)
install.packages("tidyr")
library(tidyr)
install.packages("compiler")
library(compiler)SSING - FIXED VERSION
# ============================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(compiler)

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixed CRPS Functions
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

# CRPS for parameter optimization - FIXED
crps.normal <- function(param, t.obs, t.ens, t.var, K) {
  param[-1] <- param[-1]^2
  
  # Ensure proper matrix multiplication
  if(is.matrix(t.ens)) {
    MEAN <- as.vector(t.ens %*% param[1:K])
  } else {
    MEAN <- param[1] + param[2] * t.ens
  }
  
  VAR <- param[K + 1] + param[K + 2] * t.var
  VAR[VAR <= 0] <- 1e-6  # Prevent negative variance
  SIGMA <- sqrt(VAR)
  
  obs.stdz <- (t.obs - MEAN) / SIGMA
  crps.obs <- SIGMA * (obs.stdz * (2 * pnorm(obs.stdz, 0, 1) - 1) + 
                       2 * dnorm(obs.stdz, 0, 1) - 1 / sqrt(pi))
  
  return(sum(crps.obs, na.rm = TRUE))
}
crps.normal <- cmpfun(crps.normal)

# Other CRPS functions remain the same
crps.ensemble <- function(obs, fcst) {
  fcst <- sort(fcst)
  fun.ecdf <- ecdf(fcst)
  diff <- diff(sort(c(fcst, obs)))
  cdfs <- fun.ecdf(sort(c(fcst, obs)))[1:length(fcst)]
  crps <- sum((cdfs - (fcst > obs))^2 * diff)
  return(crps)
}

crps.norm <- function(obs, mu, sd) {
  if(sd <= 0) sd <- 1e-6
  sd * ((obs - mu) / sd * (2 * pnorm((obs - mu) / sd) - 1) + 
        2 * dnorm((obs - mu) / sd) - 1 / sqrt(pi))
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simplified EMOS function
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

run_emos_simplified <- function(data, 
                               lead_time = 6,
                               training_days = 30,
                               obs_column = "T_DRYB_10") {
  
  # Filter and prepare data
  data_lt <- data %>%
    filter(lead_time_hrs == lead_time)
  
  # Aggregate by time
  data_agg <- data_lt %>%
    group_by(Valid_datetime, season) %>%
    summarise(
      ens_mean = mean(Temperature_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      ens_var = var(Temperature_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      obs = first(!!sym(obs_column)),
      ens_values = list(Temperature_C),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(obs)) %>%
    arrange(Valid_datetime)
  
  n_times <- nrow(data_agg)
  cat(sprintf("Processing %d time points with observations\n", n_times))
  
  # Initialize results
  results_list <- list()
  
  # Loop through time windows
  for(i in (training_days + 1):n_times) {
    
    # Training window
    train_idx <- (i - training_days):(i - 1)
    train_data <- data_agg[train_idx, ]
    valid_data <- data_agg[i, ]
    
    # Skip if missing data
    if(any(is.na(train_data$ens_mean)) || any(is.na(train_data$obs))) next
    
    # Fit simple linear model for initialization
    lm_fit <- lm(obs ~ ens_mean, data = train_data)
    
    # Initial parameters
    a_init <- coef(lm_fit)[1]
    b_init <- max(0.1, coef(lm_fit)[2])  # Ensure positive
    c_init <- 5
    d_init <- 1
    
    params_init <- c(a_init, sqrt(b_init), sqrt(c_init), sqrt(d_init))
    
    # Prepare optimization data
    t_obs <- train_data$obs
    t_ens <- as.matrix(cbind(1, train_data$ens_mean))
    t_var <- train_data$ens_var
    
    # Optimize
    opt_result <- tryCatch({
      optim(params_init, crps.normal,
            t.obs = t_obs,
            t.ens = t_ens,
            t.var = t_var,
            K = 2,
            method = "BFGS",
            control = list(maxit = 1000))
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(sprintf("Optimization failed at time %d: %s\n", i, e$message))
      return(NULL)
    })
    
    if(is.null(opt_result)) next
    
    # Extract parameters
    params <- opt_result$par
    params[-1] <- params[-1]^2
    
    # Make prediction
    pred_mean <- params[1] + params[2] * valid_data$ens_mean
    pred_var <- params[3] + params[4] * valid_data$ens_var
    pred_sd <- sqrt(max(pred_var, 1e-6))
    
    # Calculate CRPS
    crps_emos <- crps.norm(valid_data$obs, pred_mean, pred_sd)
    crps_raw <- crps.ensemble(valid_data$obs, unlist(valid_data$ens_values))
    
    # Store result
    results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      time = valid_data$Valid_datetime,
      season = valid_data$season,
      obs = valid_data$obs,
      pred_mean = pred_mean,
      pred_sd = pred_sd,
      crps_emos = crps_emos,
      crps_raw = crps_raw,
      improvement = crps_raw - crps_emos
    )
  }
  
  # Combine results
  if(length(results_list) > 0) {
    results <- bind_rows(results_list)
    
    # Summary
    cat("\n===== RESULTS SUMMARY =====\n")
    summary_stats <- results %>%
      group_by(season) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n(),
        mean_crps_emos = round(mean(crps_emos), 3),
        mean_crps_raw = round(mean(crps_raw), 3),
        mean_improvement = round(mean(improvement), 3),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    print(summary_stats)
    
    return(results)
  } else {
    cat("No results generated\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# Run for all observation types
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

# # Test with official observations first
# results_official <- run_emos_simplified(
#   all_seasons_2021_G818,
#   lead_time = 6,
#   training_days = 30,
#   obs_column = "T_DRYB_10"
# )
# 
# # If successful, run for WOW
# if(!is.null(results_official)) {
#   results_wow <- run_emos_simplified(
#     all_seasons_2021_G818,
#     lead_time = 6,
#     training_days = 30,
#     obs_column = "wow_temp_mean"
#   )
# }


```
#CROSS-VALIDATION
```{r}
# ============================================
#     CROSS-VALIDATION EMOS FUNCTIONS
# ============================================

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Data Preparation Function
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

prepare_cross_validation_data <- function(data, 
                                         obs_column_knmi = "T_DRYB_10",
                                         obs_column_wow = "wow_temp_mean") {
  
  # Prepare separate datasets for KNMI and WOW
  data_knmi <- data %>%
    mutate(obs_source = "KNMI",
           obs = !!sym(obs_column_knmi)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(obs))
  
  data_wow <- data %>%
    mutate(obs_source = "WOW",
           obs = !!sym(obs_column_wow)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(obs))
  
  return(list(knmi = data_knmi, wow = data_wow))
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Training Data Mixing Function
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

mix_training_data <- function(knmi_data, wow_data, 
                              mixing_ratio = 0.5,
                              strategy = "temporal") {
  
  if(strategy == "temporal") {
    # Mix by time - ensure temporal continuity
    all_times <- sort(unique(c(knmi_data$Valid_datetime, 
                               wow_data$Valid_datetime)))
    
    # Alternate data source every other time point
    knmi_times <- all_times[seq(1, length(all_times), 2)]
    wow_times <- all_times[seq(2, length(all_times), 2)]
    
    mixed_data <- rbind(
      filter(knmi_data, Valid_datetime %in% knmi_times),
      filter(wow_data, Valid_datetime %in% wow_times)
    ) %>%
      arrange(Valid_datetime)
    
  } else if(strategy == "random") {
    # Random mixing
    n_total <- min(nrow(knmi_data), nrow(wow_data))
    n_knmi <- round(n_total * mixing_ratio)
    n_wow <- n_total - n_knmi
    
    set.seed(123)  # Ensure reproducibility
    mixed_data <- rbind(
      sample_n(knmi_data, n_knmi),
      sample_n(wow_data, n_wow)
    ) %>%
      arrange(Valid_datetime)
    
  } else if(strategy == "balanced") {
    # Balanced mixing - each time point includes both data types
    common_times <- intersect(knmi_data$Valid_datetime, 
                             wow_data$Valid_datetime)
    
    mixed_data <- rbind(
      filter(knmi_data, Valid_datetime %in% common_times) %>%
        mutate(weight = mixing_ratio),
      filter(wow_data, Valid_datetime %in% common_times) %>%
        mutate(weight = 1 - mixing_ratio)
    ) %>%
      arrange(Valid_datetime)
  }
  
  return(mixed_data)
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Core Cross-Validation EMOS Function
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

run_cross_validation_emos <- function(data,
                                      train_source = "KNMI", # "KNMI", "WOW", "Mixed"
                                      test_source = "KNMI",  # "KNMI", "WOW", "Both"
                                      lead_time = 6,
                                      training_days = 30,
                                      mixing_ratio = 0.5,
                                      mixing_strategy = "temporal") {
  
  cat(sprintf("\n=== Running Cross-Validation: %s → %s ===\n", 
              train_source, test_source))
  
  # 1. Prepare data
  cv_data <- prepare_cross_validation_data(data)
  knmi_data <- cv_data$knmi
  wow_data <- cv_data$wow
  
  # 2. Select training data
  if(train_source == "KNMI") {
    train_pool <- knmi_data
  } else if(train_source == "WOW") {
    train_pool <- wow_data
  } else if(train_source == "Mixed") {
    train_pool <- mix_training_data(knmi_data, wow_data, 
                                    mixing_ratio, mixing_strategy)
  }
  
  # 3. Select test data
  test_pools <- list()
  if(test_source == "KNMI") {
    test_pools[["KNMI"]] <- knmi_data
  } else if(test_source == "WOW") {
    test_pools[["WOW"]] <- wow_data
  } else if(test_source == "Both") {
    test_pools[["KNMI"]] <- knmi_data
    test_pools[["WOW"]] <- wow_data
  }
  
  # 4. Run EMOS for each test set
  all_results <- list()
  
  for(test_name in names(test_pools)) {
    test_data <- test_pools[[test_name]]
    
    cat(sprintf("  Testing on %s data...\n", test_name))
    
    # Filter by lead time
    train_lt <- train_pool %>%
      filter(lead_time_hrs == lead_time)
    test_lt <- test_data %>%
      filter(lead_time_hrs == lead_time)
    
    # Aggregate by time
    train_agg <- aggregate_ensemble_data(train_lt)
    test_agg <- aggregate_ensemble_data(test_lt)
    
    # Find common time range
    all_times <- sort(unique(test_agg$Valid_datetime))
    n_times <- length(all_times)
    
    if(n_times < training_days + 10) {
      cat("    Insufficient data for testing\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Initialize results for this test set
    results_list <- list()
    
    # Loop through time windows
    for(i in (training_days + 1):n_times) {
      
      # Define training window
      test_time <- all_times[i]
      train_times <- all_times[(i - training_days):(i - 1)]
      
      # Get training data
      train_window <- train_agg %>%
        filter(Valid_datetime %in% train_times)
      
      # Get test point
      test_point <- test_agg %>%
        filter(Valid_datetime == test_time)
      
      if(nrow(train_window) < 20 || nrow(test_point) == 0) next
      
      # Optimize EMOS parameters
      emos_params <- optimize_emos_params(train_window)
      
      if(is.null(emos_params)) next
      
      # Make prediction
      pred <- predict_emos(test_point, emos_params)
      
      # Calculate scores
      crps_emos <- crps.norm(test_point$obs, pred$mean, pred$sd)
      crps_raw <- crps.ensemble(test_point$obs, unlist(test_point$ens_values))
      
      # Store results
      results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        time = test_time,
        season = test_point$season,
        train_source = train_source,
        test_source = test_name,
        obs = test_point$obs,
        pred_mean = pred$mean,
        pred_sd = pred$sd,
        crps_emos = crps_emos,
        crps_raw = crps_raw,
        improvement = crps_raw - crps_emos,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    
    if(length(results_list) > 0) {
      all_results[[test_name]] <- bind_rows(results_list)
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all test results
  if(length(all_results) > 0) {
    final_results <- bind_rows(all_results)
    return(final_results)
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Helper Functions
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

aggregate_ensemble_data <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    group_by(Valid_datetime, season) %>%
    summarise(
      ens_mean = mean(Temperature_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      ens_var = var(Temperature_C, na.rm = TRUE),
      obs = first(obs),
      ens_values = list(Temperature_C),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(obs), !is.na(ens_mean), !is.na(ens_var)) %>%
    arrange(Valid_datetime)
}

optimize_emos_params <- function(train_data) {
  
  # Prepare for optimization
  lm_fit <- tryCatch({
    lm(obs ~ ens_mean, data = train_data)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if(is.null(lm_fit)) return(NULL)
  
  # Initial parameters
  a_init <- coef(lm_fit)[1]
  b_init <- max(0.1, coef(lm_fit)[2])
  c_init <- 5
  d_init <- 1
  
  params_init <- c(a_init, sqrt(b_init), sqrt(c_init), sqrt(d_init))
  
  # Prepare data
  t_obs <- train_data$obs
  t_ens <- as.matrix(cbind(1, train_data$ens_mean))
  t_var <- train_data$ens_var
  
  # Optimize
  opt_result <- tryCatch({
    optim(params_init, crps.normal,
          t.obs = t_obs,
          t.ens = t_ens,
          t.var = t_var,
          K = 2,
          method = "BFGS",
          control = list(maxit = 1000))
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if(is.null(opt_result)) return(NULL)
  
  # Extract parameters
  params <- opt_result$par
  params[-1] <- params[-1]^2
  
  return(params)
}

predict_emos <- function(test_data, params) {
  pred_mean <- params[1] + params[2] * test_data$ens_mean
  pred_var <- params[3] + params[4] * test_data$ens_var
  pred_sd <- sqrt(max(pred_var, 1e-6))
  
  return(list(mean = pred_mean, sd = pred_sd))
}

# -------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Main Function to Run All Experiments
# -------------------------------------------------------------------

run_all_cross_validation_experiments <- function(data, 
                                                 lead_time = 6,
                                                 training_days = 30) {
  
  # Define all experiments
  experiments <- list(
    list(name = "KNMI→KNMI", train = "KNMI", test = "KNMI"),
    list(name = "WOW→WOW", train = "WOW", test = "WOW"),
    list(name = "KNMI→WOW", train = "KNMI", test = "WOW"),
    list(name = "WOW→KNMI", train = "WOW", test = "KNMI"),
    list(name = "Mixed→Both", train = "Mixed", test = "Both")
  )
  
  # Store all results
  all_results <- list()
  
  # Run each experiment
  for(exp in experiments) {
    cat(sprintf("\n========================================\n"))
    cat(sprintf("Experiment: %s\n", exp$name))
    cat(sprintf("========================================\n"))
    
    results <- run_cross_validation_emos(
      data = data,
      train_source = exp$train,
      test_source = exp$test,
      lead_time = lead_time,
      training_days = training_days
    )
    
    if(!is.null(results)) {
      results$experiment <- exp$name
      all_results[[exp$name]] <- results
    }
  }
  
  # Combine results
  if(length(all_results) > 0) {
    combined_results <- bind_rows(all_results)
    
    # Generate summary
    summary_results <- combined_results %>%
      group_by(experiment, train_source, test_source, season) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n(),
        mean_crps_emos = round(mean(crps_emos, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
        mean_crps_raw = round(mean(crps_raw, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
        mean_improvement = round(mean(improvement, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
        improvement_pct = round(mean_improvement / mean_crps_raw * 100, 1),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    
    return(list(
      detailed = combined_results,
      summary = summary_results
    ))
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
}
```

#PEV and visualisation
```{r}
# Main PEV calculation function
calculate_pev_single <- function(obs, pred_mean, pred_sd, threshold, cost_loss_ratio) {
  
  # Remove NAs
  valid_idx <- !is.na(obs) & !is.na(pred_mean) & !is.na(pred_sd)
  obs <- obs[valid_idx]
  pred_mean <- pred_mean[valid_idx]
  pred_sd <- pred_sd[valid_idx]
  n <- length(obs)
  
  if(n < 10) return(NA)
  
  # Calculate forecast probability of exceeding threshold
  prob_exceed <- 1 - pnorm(threshold, pred_mean, pred_sd)
  
  # Binary decision
  take_action <- prob_exceed > cost_loss_ratio
  
  # Observed event
  event_occurred <- obs > threshold
  
  # Contingency table
  hits <- sum(take_action & event_occurred)
  false_alarms <- sum(take_action & !event_occurred)
  misses <- sum(!take_action & event_occurred)
  
  # Observed relative frequency
  orf <- sum(event_occurred) / n
  
  if(orf == 0 || orf == 1) return(0)
  
  # Calculate expenses
  expense_forecast <- (hits + false_alarms) * cost_loss_ratio + misses * 1
  
  # Climatology expense
  if(cost_loss_ratio < orf) {
    expense_clim <- n * cost_loss_ratio
  } else {
    expense_clim <- sum(event_occurred) * 1
  }
  
  # Perfect forecast expense
  expense_perfect <- sum(event_occurred) * cost_loss_ratio
  
  # PEV calculation
  if(abs(expense_clim - expense_perfect) < 1e-10) {
    pev <- 0
  } else {
    pev <- (expense_clim - expense_forecast) / (expense_clim - expense_perfect)
  }
  
  return(pev)
}

# Calculate PEV curves for multiple experiments
calculate_pev_curves <- function(cv_results, 
                                use_percentiles = TRUE,
                                percentiles = c(75, 85, 90, 95),
                                fixed_thresholds = c(15, 20, 25, 30)) {
  
  # Cost-loss ratios to test
  cl_ratios <- seq(0.01, 0.50, by = 0.02)
  
  # Get all unique experiments
  experiments <- unique(cv_results$detailed$experiment)
  seasons <- unique(cv_results$detailed$season)
  
  all_pev_results <- list()
  
  for(exp in experiments) {
    for(seas in seasons) {
      
      # Filter data
      exp_data <- cv_results$detailed %>%
        filter(experiment == exp, season == seas,
               !is.na(obs), !is.na(pred_mean), !is.na(pred_sd))
      
      if(nrow(exp_data) < 30) next
      
      # Determine thresholds
      if(use_percentiles) {
        thresholds <- quantile(exp_data$obs, percentiles/100, na.rm = TRUE)
        names(thresholds) <- paste0("P", percentiles)
      } else {
        # Adjust for actual temperature scale
        # Since your data shows max ~30°C in reality, adjust thresholds
        obs_max <- max(exp_data$obs, na.rm = TRUE)
        obs_min <- min(exp_data$obs, na.rm = TRUE)
        
        # Scale thresholds to actual data range
        thresholds <- fixed_thresholds[fixed_thresholds > obs_min & 
                                       fixed_thresholds < obs_max]
        if(length(thresholds) == 0) {
          # Use percentiles as fallback
          thresholds <- quantile(exp_data$obs, c(0.75, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE)
        }
      }
      
      # Calculate PEV for each threshold
      for(i in 1:length(thresholds)) {
        thresh <- thresholds[i]
        thresh_name <- names(thresholds)[i]
        
        pev_values <- sapply(cl_ratios, function(cl) {
          calculate_pev_single(
            obs = exp_data$obs,
            pred_mean = exp_data$pred_mean,
            pred_sd = exp_data$pred_sd,
            threshold = thresh,
            cost_loss_ratio = cl
          )
        })
        
        # Store results
        all_pev_results[[length(all_pev_results) + 1]] <- data.frame(
          experiment = exp,
          season = seas,
          threshold = thresh,
          threshold_name = thresh_name,
          cost_loss_ratio = cl_ratios,
          pev = pev_values,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(bind_rows(all_pev_results))
}

# Calculate integrated PEV (area under curve)
calculate_integrated_pev <- function(pev_curves) {
  
  integrated <- pev_curves %>%
    group_by(experiment, season, threshold_name) %>%
    summarise(
      integrated_pev = {
        valid_idx <- !is.na(pev)
        if(sum(valid_idx) < 2) {
          NA
        } else {
          x <- cost_loss_ratio[valid_idx]
          y <- pev[valid_idx]
          sum(diff(x) * (y[-1] + y[-length(y)]) / 2)
        }
      },
      max_pev = max(pev, na.rm = TRUE),
      optimal_cl = cost_loss_ratio[which.max(pev)],
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  return(integrated)
}

# Visualization function
plot_pev_comparison <- function(pev_curves, 
                                selected_season = "Summer",
                                selected_threshold = "P90") {
  
  # Filter data
  plot_data <- pev_curves %>%
    filter(season == selected_season,
           threshold_name == selected_threshold)
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = cost_loss_ratio, y = pev, 
                             color = experiment, linetype = experiment)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_point(data = plot_data %>% group_by(experiment) %>% 
                 slice_max(pev, n = 1),
               size = 3) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.2, 1)) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("PEV Curves - %s (%s)", selected_season, selected_threshold),
      subtitle = "Higher values indicate better economic value",
      x = "Cost/Loss Ratio",
      y = "Potential Economic Value",
      color = "Experiment",
      linetype = "Experiment"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  return(p)
}

# Create summary heatmap
plot_pev_heatmap <- function(integrated_pev, threshold_name = "P90") {
  
  # Filter and prepare data
  heatmap_data <- integrated_pev %>%
    filter(threshold_name == threshold_name) %>%
    mutate(
      # Shorten experiment labels to avoid overlap
      experiment_short = case_when(
        grepl("KNMI→KNMI", experiment) ~ "KNMI→KNMI",
        grepl("WOW→WOW", experiment) ~ "WOW→WOW",
        grepl("KNMI→WOW", experiment) ~ "KNMI→WOW",
        grepl("WOW→KNMI", experiment) ~ "WOW→KNMI",
        grepl("Mixed→KNMI", experiment) ~ "Mixed→KNMI",
        grepl("Mixed→WOW", experiment) ~ "Mixed→WOW",
        TRUE ~ experiment
      )
    )
  
  # Create plot with adjusted dimensions
  p <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = season, y = experiment_short, 
                                fill = integrated_pev)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", integrated_pev)), 
              color = "black", size = 3) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = "red", mid = "yellow", high = "green",
      midpoint = 0.25,
      name = "Integrated\nPEV",
      limits = c(0, 0.5)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("Integrated PEV (%s threshold)", threshold_name),
      subtitle = "Area under PEV curve - Higher is better",
      x = "Season",
      y = NULL  # Remove y-axis label
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 1),  # Adjust y-axis text
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 50),  # Add left margin for labels
      panel.grid = element_blank()  # Remove grid for cleaner look
    ) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 0.5)  # Adjust aspect ratio
  
  return(p)
}


# Main analysis function
run_pev_analysis <- function(cv_results) {
  
  cat("\n========== PEV ANALYSIS ==========\n")
  
  # Calculate PEV curves
  cat("Calculating PEV curves...\n")
  pev_curves <- calculate_pev_curves(cv_results, use_percentiles = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate integrated PEV
  cat("Calculating integrated PEV...\n")
  integrated_pev <- calculate_integrated_pev(pev_curves)
  
  # Print summary
  cat("\n===== INTEGRATED PEV SUMMARY =====\n")
  summary_table <- integrated_pev %>%
    filter(threshold_name %in% c("P75", "P90")) %>%
    select(experiment, season, threshold_name, integrated_pev, max_pev, optimal_cl) %>%
    arrange(experiment, season, threshold_name)
  
  print(summary_table)
  
  # Create visualizations
  cat("\nCreating visualizations...\n")
  
  # Plot for each season
  plots <- list()
  for(seas in unique(pev_curves$season)) {
    plots[[seas]] <- plot_pev_comparison(pev_curves, seas, "P90")
  }
  
  # Heatmap
  heatmap <- plot_pev_heatmap(integrated_pev, "P90")
  
  # Save results
  write.csv(integrated_pev, "pev_integrated_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  
  return(list(
    curves = pev_curves,
    integrated = integrated_pev,
    plots = plots,
    heatmap = heatmap
  ))
}


```

# Enhanced cross-validation with PEV
```{r}
# Enhanced cross-validation with PEV
run_cross_validation_with_pev <- function(data,
                                          train_source = "KNMI",
                                          test_source = "KNMI",
                                          lead_time = 6,
                                          training_days = 30,
                                          threshold_percentiles = c(75, 90, 95)) {
  
  # Run standard cross-validation first
  cv_results <- run_cross_validation_emos(
    data = data,
    train_source = train_source,
    test_source = test_source,
    lead_time = lead_time,
    training_days = training_days
  )
  
  if(is.null(cv_results)) return(NULL)
  
  # Calculate PEV for different thresholds
  pev_results <- list()
  
  for(thresh_pct in threshold_percentiles) {
    
    # Group by experiment and season
    grouped_results <- cv_results %>%
      group_by(experiment, season, train_source, test_source) %>%
      nest()
    
    # Calculate PEV for each group
    pev_summary <- grouped_results %>%
      mutate(
        pev_curve = map(data, ~calculate_pev_curve(.x, thresh_pct)),
        integrated_pev = map_dbl(pev_curve, calculate_integrated_pev),
        threshold_pct = thresh_pct
      )
    
    pev_results[[paste0("thresh_", thresh_pct)]] <- pev_summary
  }
  
  # Combine PEV results
  all_pev <- bind_rows(pev_results) %>%
    select(-data, -pev_curve)  # Remove nested columns for summary
  
  return(list(
    cv_results = cv_results,
    pev_summary = all_pev
  ))
}
```
